app.name: Ji-Cheng
onboarding.welcome.title: Welcome to Ji-Cheng
onboarding.welcome.subtitle: A modern Git version control experience
onboarding.login.title: Log in to GitHub
onboarding.login.description: Sign in to access Pull Requests and code review features
onboarding.analytics.title: Help Improve Ji-Cheng
onboarding.analytics.description: Send anonymous usage data to help us improve
onboarding.welcome.addLocalProjectDesc: Should be a valid git repository
onboarding.welcome.cloneRepoDesc: Clone a repo using a URL
onboarding.clone.title: Clone a repository
onboarding.clone.validationError: You must add both a repository URL and target directory.
onboarding.projectSetup.targetBranchDesc: Your main "production" branch, typically origin/master or upstream/main.
onboarding.projectSetup.multipleRemotesD: You have branches from multiple remotes. If you want to specify a remote for creating branches that is different from the remote that your target branch is on, change it here.
onboarding.projectSetup.gerritDesc: It looks like this project might be a Gerrit project. Do you want to enable Gerrit mode? You can adjust this later in the project settings if needed.
onboarding.projectSetup.workspaceInfoTit: GitButler switches your active branch to gitbutler/workspace
onboarding.projectSetup.workspaceInfoDes: In order to support working on multiple branches simultaneously, GitButler creates and automatically manages a special branch gitbutler/workspace. You can always switch back and forth as needed between normal git branches and the Gitbutler workspace.
onboarding.projectNotFound.description: Sorry, we can't find the project you're looking for.
onboarding.projectNotFound.removed: It might have been removed or doesn't exist.
onboarding.analytics.telemetryDesc: GitButler uses telemetry strictly to help us improve the client. We do not collect any personal information, unless explicitly allowed below.
onboarding.analytics.feedbackDesc: We kindly ask you to consider keeping these settings enabled as it helps us catch issues more quickly. If you choose to disable them, please feel free to share your feedback on our Discord.
onboarding.analytics.errorReportingDesc: Toggle reporting of application crashes and errors.
onboarding.login.detected: A new login attempt has been detected for the user with email {email}. Would you like to accept this login?
workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.discardConfirm: Are you sure you want to discard these changes?
branch.name: Branch
branch.card.createOrDragDrop: Create or drag & drop commits here.
branch.card.noCommitsYet: There are no commits yet on this branch.
branch.details.conflictDescription: GitButler opens the earliest commit first, since later commits depend on it.
branch.addDependentBranch.title: Add dependent branch
branch.renameModal.title: Rename branch
branch.renameModal.pushedWarning: Renaming a branch that has already been pushed will create a new branch at the remote. The old one will remain untouched but will be disassociated from this branch.
branch.deleteModal.title: Delete branch
branch.deleteModal.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {branchName}?
branch.draftHeader.description: A new branch will be created for your {mode}.
branch.draftHeader.clickToRename: Click the name to rename it now or later.
branch.createModal.independentDesc:
Create an independent branch
in a new stack.
branch.createModal.dependentDescNone:
Create a branch that depends
on another stack (none available).
branch.createModal.dependentDescAvailabl:
Create a branch that depends
on a selected stack.
branch.createModal.stackInfo: The new branch will be applied in parallel with other stacks in the workspace.
branch.createModal.adjustPlacement: Adjust branch placement and preferences in
branch.createModal.dependentInfo: Creates a branch that depends on a selected stack.
branch.createModal.stackTopBranches: A stack's top branches also have a
branch.integrationModal.title: Update {branchName}
branch.integrationModal.divergedDescript: The local branch and its remote counterpart have diverged.
You can review the divergence between them, decide if and how to integrate the changes into your local branch, preview and apply the changes.
branch.integrationModal.usingStrategySte: Using the selected strategy steps. Expand to inspect or edit them.
branch.integrationModal.runPreview: Run preview to inspect the resulting branch shape.
branch.integrationModal.previewNoSegment: Preview produced no branch segment for this ref.
branch.integrationModal.previewEmptyBran: The resulting branch would be empty.
branch.integrationModal.desc.pullRebase: Rebuilds the branch picking first the commits on the remote, and then the commits on the local branch.
branch.integrationModal.desc.smartSquash: Tries to fold matching remote work into related local commits. This is done through matching Change IDs, and falling back to pull-rebase ordering otherwise.
branch.integrationModal.desc.merge: Keeps your local history and merges the remote tip into it.
branch.integrationModal.desc.pickRemote: Rebuilds the branch picking only the commits on the remote.
branch.integrationSteps.noSteps: No integration steps yet. Add a step to build the plan.
commit.title: Commits
commit.author: Author
commit.details.author: Author
commit.cherryApplyModal.title: Cherry-pick commit
commit.cherryApplyModal.applicableToAnyS: This commit can be applied to any stack. Select a stack below.
commit.cherryApplyModal.lockedToStack: This commit conflicts when applied to the selected stack, as such it must be applied to the selected stack to avoid a workspace conflict.
commit.cherryApplyModal.causesWorkspaceC: This commit can't be applied since it would cause a workspace conflict.
commit.cherryApplyModal.noStacks: No stacks are currently applied to the workspace.
commit.failedModal.someChangesNotCommitt: was created, but some changes weren't fully committed:
commit.failedModal.couldNotCreateCommit: Commit could not be created because of the following reasons:
commit.editPatchConfirmModal.title: Resolve conflicts to preview
commit.editPatchConfirmModal.description: The file {fileName} has unresolved merge conflicts that need to be addressed before it can be previewed.
diff.title: Diff
settings.title: Settings
conflict.title: Conflict Resolution
forge.pr.title: Pull Request
forge.review.title: Code Review
history.title: History
error.loadFailedDesc: An error occurred while loading data
error.gitErrorDesc: A git operation failed. Check the error details
error.networkErrorDesc: Network connection failed. Check your network settings
error.unknownDesc: An unknown error occurred. Please try again
rules.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this rule? This action cannot be undone.
share.title: Share debug data with Ji-Cheng team for review
share.description: If you are having trouble, please share your project and logs with the Ji-Cheng team. We will review it for you and help identify how we can help resolve the issue.
share.emailPlaceholder: Provide an email so that we can get back to you
share.commentsPlaceholder: Provide any steps necessary to reproduce the problem.
share.logsDescription: We personally ensure all information you share with us will be reviewed internally only and discarded post-resolution
absorb.title: Absorb Changes into Commits
absorb.description: The following changes will be absorbed into their respective commits:
absorb.noCommitsFound: No suitable commits found to absorb changes into.
lineLocks.dependsOnStacks: This line depends on changes inside the following stacks
lineLocks.dependsOnStack: This line depends on changes inside
lineLocks.dependsOnUnknown: This line depends on changes inside an unidentifiable stack
integrateUpstream.resolveDivergence: Resolve target branch divergence before updating the workspace.
integrateUpstream.uncommittedConflictDes: These files will conflict when your current uncommitted changes are applied onto the updated workspace.
integrateUpstream.uncommittedConflictCon: You're free to proceed, but conflict markers will be added to your uncommitted work.
integrateUpstreamDeprecated.deprecatedDe: This is the older modal, using deprecated APIs. Please consider enabling the new integration modal in the experimental settings.
integrateUpstreamDeprecated.conflictingD: Updating the workspace will add conflict markers to the following files.
integrateUpstreamDeprecated.selectAction: Select an action to proceed with updating.
branchesViewPR.description: To apply a branch from a fork, GitButler must first add the fork as a remote.
aiSettings.aboutText: Ji-Cheng supports multiple AI providers: OpenAI and Anthropic (via API or your own key), OpenRouter for access to hundreds of models, plus local models through Ollama and LM Studio.
aiSettings.signInApi: Please sign in to use the Ji-Cheng API.
aiSettings.butlerApiNoteOpenAI: Ji-Cheng uses OpenAI API for commit messages and branch names.
aiSettings.butlerApiNoteAnthropic: Ji-Cheng uses Anthropic API for commit messages and branch names.
aiSettings.ollamaCspNote: To connect to your Ollama endpoint, allow-list it in the app's CSP settings.
aiSettings.lmStudioIntro: Connecting to your LM Studio endpoint requires that you do two things:
aiSettings.lmStudioCsp: 1. Allow-list it in the CSP settings for the application. You can find more details on how to do that in the Ji-Cheng docs.
aiSettings.lmStudioCors: 2. Enable CORS support in LM Studio. You can find more details on how to do that in the LM Studio docs.
aiSettings.customPromptsDesc: Ji-Cheng's AI assistant generates commit messages and branch names. Use default prompts or create your own. Assign prompts in the project settings.
aiSettings.diffLengthCaption: How many characters of your git diff should be provided to AI
credentialCheck.problem: There was a problem with your credentials
credentialCheck.helpText: Try another setting and test again?
credentialCheck.helpLink: Consult our <Link href="https://docs.gitbutler.com/troubleshooting/fetch-push">fetch / push guide</Link> for help fixing this problem.
credentialCheck.disclaimer: To test the push command, we create an empty branch and promptly remove it after the check. <Link href="https://docs.gitbutler.com/troubleshooting/fetch-push">Read more</Link> about authentication methods.
commitSigning.caption: Use GPG or SSH to sign your commits so they can be verified as authentic.
commitSigning.gitButlerNote: Ji-Cheng will sign commits as per your git configuration, but evaluates
commitSigning.disclaimer: Signing commits can allow other people to verify your commits if you publish the public version of your signing key. <Link href="https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/virtual-branches/signing-commits">Read more</Link> about commit signing and verification.
cloudForm.description: Ji-Cheng supports the use of OpenAI and Anthropic to provide commit message and branch name generation. This works either through Ji-Cheng's API or in a bring your own key configuration and can be configured in the main preferences screen.
cloudForm.enableBranchGen: Enable branch and commit message generation
cloudForm.enableBranchGenCaption: If enabled, diffs will be sent to OpenAI or Anthropic's servers when pressing the "Generate message" and "Generate branch name" button.
cloudForm.enableExperimentalCaption: If enabled, you will be able to access the AI features currently in development. This also requires you to use OpenAI through Ji-Cheng in order for the features to work.
cloudForm.customPromptsDesc: You can apply your own custom prompts to the project. By default, the project uses Ji-Cheng prompts, but you can create your own prompts in the general settings.
forgeAccountConfig.caption: Enable {requestType} creation. Read more in the
gerritForm.title: Gerrit configuration
gerritForm.caption: Enable or disable Gerrit mode for this project. <Link href="https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/gerrit-mode">Learn more</Link>
